key_insight: Interpretable linear meta-learning, combined with adaptive uncertainty calibration, can efficiently guide multi-objective molecular search toward better Pareto-optimal candidates with limited data.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical, scalable way to accelerate chemical discovery while keeping surrogate models interpretable and suitable for computationally expensive quantum-level evaluations.
method: Meta-train linear surrogate models across chemical objectives and cheap auxiliary properties to transfer useful chemical priors.
Embed the surrogates in an Efficient Global Optimization (EGO) loop for multi-objective search.
Use adaptive confidence tuning to recalibrate uncertainty and balance exploration vs. exploitation as the search progresses.
evidence_signal: On a large-scale spin-crossover metal-organic complex search, the baseline was 78% worse in Pareto sense than the meta-learning approach, and dynamic confidence tuning improved over 50% of the statically calibrated front.
watch_item: Performance depends on reliable uncertainty calibration; if tail behavior is misestimated, active-search decisions may still be biased toward poor exploration or premature exploitation.
paper_title: Interpretable Meta-Learning for Multi-Objective Chemical Search
